stories.title: The Lost Kitten
stories.story:
One rainy afternoon, Omar was walking home from school when he heard a tiny, sad meow coming from behind a trash can. He looked closer and found a small, wet kitten shivering in the cold. It looked hungry and completely alone.

Omar couldn't just leave it there. He gently picked up the kitten, wrapped it in his jacket, and carried it home. His mother wasn't sure at first — they had never had a pet before — but when she saw how gentle Omar was with the tiny animal, she agreed to let it stay, at least for one night.

Over the next few days, Omar knocked on every neighbor's door, asking if they had lost a kitten. Nobody knew where it came from. Slowly, the kitten grew stronger, and it began following Omar everywhere around the house, purring whenever he came near.

After two weeks with no owner found, Omar's mother smiled and said, "I think this kitten has already decided where it belongs." Omar named the kitten Lucky, and from that day on, they were inseparable best friends.

stories.title: The Science Fair Disaster
stories.story:
Hana had spent three whole weeks building her volcano model for the school science fair. She had painted every rock carefully and mixed the perfect chemicals so it would erupt with real red foam. She was certain she would win first place.

On the morning of the science fair, disaster struck. While carrying her volcano to the car, Hana tripped on the front step, and the model shattered into pieces on the ground. She sat there in shock, tears filling her eyes, sure that her project was completely ruined.

Her father knelt down beside her. "We have twenty minutes before we need to leave," he said calmly. "Let's see what we can fix." Together, they glued the broken pieces back together as quickly as they could. It wasn't perfect anymore — you could see cracks along the sides — but it still worked.

At the fair, Hana nervously explained her project, cracks and all. To her surprise, the judges were impressed not just by the volcano, but by how she calmly explained what had happened and fixed it under pressure. She didn't win first place, but she won something better: the confidence that she could handle any disaster that came her way.
